target.name: Locus Robotics
target.founder: Bruce Welty, Rick Faulk, Al Dekin

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $2.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $2.3B.

private.name: Augury
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series F)

data.narrative:
Locus Robotics, a Series F robotics and manufacturing company, has raised $438M to date against a current post-money valuation of $2.0B. The valuation assessment relies on a peer universe of 8 comparable companies—1 public and 7 private—spanning similar stage and sector positioning. Key private comparables include Divergent ($2.3B, Series E), Exotec ($2.0B, Series D), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B, Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B, Series B), and Hadrian ($1.6B, Series C).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, applying a 1.3x step-up multiplier typical of Series F advancement, implies a value of $2.6B. Peer-median benchmarking across the 6-company comparable set yields $1.7B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $2.3B. The divergence reflects both the company's more advanced stage relative to the median peer cohort and heterogeneity in early-stage robotics valuations.

Triangulating these methods produces a valuation range of $1.4B (low) to $3.0B (high), with a median of $2.1B. The current $2.0B valuation sits near the lower bound of the triangulated range. Key uncertainties include revenue scale relative to peer companies—not provided in this analysis—and the timing and terms of any subsequent financing, which would materially influence both stage-progression assumptions and peer comparability.
